content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed both pending amendments for planned-elsewhere reconciliation correctness. Amendment 1: Removed redundant prompt re-reads from engine.rs:record_planned_elsewhere_amendments and success_reconciliation.rs:reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings. The authoritative PE validation in final_review.rs (lines 644-656 and 1526-1536) already strips invalid mapped_to_bead_id values before acceptance — the aggregate committed to disk contains only validated data. The downstream re-reads in engine.rs (allowed_pe_ids block at lines 7614-7627 and validation check at lines 7638-7657) and success_reconciliation.rs (allowed_pe_ids block at lines 968-984 and validation check at lines 1012-1016) were fail-closed duplicates that could silently reject valid mappings if the mutable prompt changed or became unreadable between final-review and reconciliation. Removed both blocks from both files, cleaned up now-unused imports (FsProjectStore, ProjectStorePort, task_prompt_contract, FileSystem from top-level import) in success_reconciliation.rs, and removed the project record and prompt file setup from the reconstruct_pe_mappings_deduplicates_by_round_latest_wins test that was only needed for the now-removed validation. Amendment 2: Fixed the milestone-state loader so later completion rounds with zero planned-elsewhere mappings can still supersede earlier rounds. Three coordinated changes: (a) Added record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel() to service.rs that writes a ProgressUpdated journal event with sub_type='pe_round_sentinel' carrying active_bead_id, run_id, and completion_round metadata — marking that this round was processed even with zero PE mappings. (b) Modified record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs to always write a PE round sentinel before processing amendments (removed the early return on empty amendments), and removed the if-!is_empty guards at both call sites (Complete path line 2705 and Restart path line 3086) so the function is always called. (c) Updated rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal in service.rs to collect pe_round_sentinel events into a sentinel_max_round HashMap during the event scan, then seed max_round_by_bead_run from it before merging mapping-derived rounds. A round-2 sentinel with zero PE events now correctly supersedes round-1 mappings.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1a: Removed the allowed_pe_ids prompt re-read block (lines 7614-7627) and the validation check with warning log (lines 7638-7657) from record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs. Replaced with a comment noting that PE validation is authoritative in final_review.rs. Kept the trim/empty normalization check as input sanitization.
2. [Completed] Amendment 1b: Removed the allowed_pe_ids prompt re-read block (lines 968-984) and the validation check (lines 1012-1016) from reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings in success_reconciliation.rs. Replaced with a comment noting that the aggregate already contains validated data from final_review.rs.
3. [Completed] Amendment 1c: Cleaned up now-unused imports in success_reconciliation.rs: removed FsProjectStore from the fs import group, removed the entire ProjectStorePort and task_prompt_contract import lines, removed the unused ArtifactStorePort import, and removed the unused FileSystem from the top-level import (it was only used at the removed line 973; other usages are fully qualified or in #[cfg(test)] blocks with their own imports).
4. [Completed] Amendment 1d: Removed the project record and prompt file setup block from the reconstruct_pe_mappings_deduplicates_by_round_latest_wins test (lines 3074-3096) since the PE validation it supported was removed.
5. [Completed] Amendment 2a: Added record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel() public function to service.rs. Writes a ProgressUpdated journal event with sub_type='pe_round_sentinel' metadata containing active_bead_id, run_id, and completion_round. Uses the existing MilestoneJournalPort for journal writes.
6. [Completed] Amendment 2b: Modified record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs: removed the early return on empty amendments. Added a PE round sentinel write (via milestone_service::record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel) before the amendment processing loop, so every invocation records the round regardless of PE amendment count.
7. [Completed] Amendment 2c: Removed the if-!pe_amendments.is_empty() guard at the Complete path call site (line 2705) and the if-!planned_elsewhere.is_empty() guard at the Restart path call site (line 3086), so record_planned_elsewhere_amendments is always called. Updated comments to explain that always-calling ensures a PE round sentinel is written for correct round supersession.
8. [Completed] Amendment 2d: Updated rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal in service.rs to detect pe_round_sentinel events during the journal scan. Added a sentinel_max_round HashMap that collects (active_bead_id, run_id) -> max completion_round from sentinel events. Seeded max_round_by_bead_run from sentinel_max_round before merging mapping-derived rounds, so zero-PE rounds properly inform the supersession filter.
9. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix three minor formatting issues: collapsed multi-line import in success_reconciliation.rs, collapsed single-expression metadata.insert in service.rs, and reformatted MilestoneJournalEvent chain in service.rs.
10. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (689 lib, 174 integration, 1 tmux, 758 unit, 9 doc-tests ignored)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (engine.rs, success_reconciliation.rs, service.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: engine.rs record_planned_elsewhere_amendments no longer contains allowed_pe_ids block or validation check — the loop directly processes amendments after trim/empty normalization. success_reconciliation.rs reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings no longer contains allowed_pe_ids block or validation check — the loop directly builds identity keys and writes to journal. The authoritative validation remains in final_review.rs lines 644-656 (before voter/arbiter prompts) and lines 1526-1536 (defense-in-depth on final_accepted_amendments).
- Amendment 1 verified: grep confirms FsProjectStore, ProjectStorePort, task_prompt_contract, and top-level FileSystem are no longer imported in success_reconciliation.rs (test blocks have their own FileSystem imports). ArtifactStorePort is retained as it is used by FsArtifactStore.list_payloads at line 914.
- Amendment 2 verified: record_planned_elsewhere_round_sentinel writes ProgressUpdated event with sub_type='pe_round_sentinel' metadata. rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal detects these events via sub_type check and populates sentinel_max_round. max_round_by_bead_run is initialized from sentinel_max_round (not empty HashMap), so a round-2 sentinel with zero PE mappings correctly sets max_round=2 and filters out round-1 mappings.
- Amendment 2 verified: Both call sites in engine.rs (Complete path and Restart path) now unconditionally call record_planned_elsewhere_amendments. The function writes a sentinel before the amendment loop, so even an empty amendment list produces a sentinel event.
- git diff --stat HEAD -- src/: 3 files changed — engine.rs (removed prompt re-read, removed empty guards, added sentinel call), success_reconciliation.rs (removed prompt re-read and validation, cleaned imports, simplified test), service.rs (added sentinel function, sentinel processing in rebuild)
- Files modified: src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The success_reconciliation.rs papering-over at lines 741-759 (authoritative_max_round from aggregates) remains as defense-in-depth — it is now redundant with the sentinel-based fix in service.rs but harmless to keep.
- The PE round sentinel writes one additional journal event per completion round even when PE amendments exist. This is a negligible overhead since the event is small and completion rounds are bounded.
- The sentinel only covers rounds that reach the record_planned_elsewhere_amendments call site (after stage commit succeeds). If the stage commit itself fails, no sentinel is written — which is correct since the round did not commit.
